title: Wrong repository name if the image is previously built with same name with one more level
body:
### Issue Description

A image is already built with repository `foo/bar`, if image is built again with a different repository `bar`, the repository is still `foo/bar`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a simple `Dockerfile`

```
FROM busybox:1.36
ENTRYPOINT ["tail", "-f", "/dev/null"]
```

2. Run `podman build -t foo/bar .`
3. Run `podman build -t bar .`


### Describe the results you received

Run `podman images` and 1 image with repository `localhost/foo/bar` is shown.


### Describe the results you expected

Run `podman images` and 2 images with repository `localhost/foo/bar` and `localhost/bar` are shown.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I upgraded with brew and `podman --version` is showing 4.6.0 but `podman info` still shows `4.5.1`.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Transferring the issue to Buildah.  So what's happening is that Buildah seems to resolve the input name before and will use the resolved name.  Otherwise, it'll normalize the name.
